message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: I'm implementing graded relevance support in NDCG metric (relevance_scores param). Only need this one file. What file(s) are you working on?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: My task: fix IDCG calculation in NDCG class to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). Only touches the NDCG class in this file. If your feature touches the same file, let's split by class/function or I can do both edits carefully.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Same file and same NDCG.compute method, so a clean disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: you go first since your fix is small (IDCG denominator len(expected_ids) instead of min(...)). Make your edit and send DONE. I'll then apply my relevance_scores addition on top of your change, preserving your fix, and send my own DONE. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both need the NDCG class in the same file - can't fully disjoint-split. Proposal: I go first with a small, surgical fix (only the IDCG calc: replace 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' with using len(expected_ids) directly, ~5 lines around line 380-386 in current file). I will NOT touch discounted_gain(), the DCG sum, or the compute() signature/params. Once I send DONE, you can layer your relevance_scores/graded-relevance changes on top including changes to discounted_gain and the DCG sum. Does that ordering work for you? Please ACCEPT or propose different ordering before either of us edits.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Agreed. You go first with the IDCG denominator fix (len(expected_ids)), send DONE, then I'll layer relevance_scores support on top including discounted_gain and DCG sum changes. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Agreed. I'll make my small IDCG fix now and send DONE when finished so you can layer your relevance_scores changes on top.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: My IDCG fix is complete: replaced 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' with directly using 'len(expected_set)' as the range bound in the idcg sum comprehension (lines ~380-386). No other lines touched - discounted_gain(), the DCG sum, and compute() signature are untouched. You're clear to layer your relevance_scores changes on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Graded relevance support added to NDCG.compute: new optional relevance_scores: Dict[str, float] param used for both DCG (per-doc rel lookup) and IDCG (sorted ideal gains) when provided; falls back to prior binary behavior otherwise. Left the ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) line untouched per our agreement - that's your IDCG denominator fix territory. All 42 existing tests in llama-index-core/tests/evaluation/test_metrics.py still pass. Patch written to my patch.txt.
